title: ContractReview
description: Output of the contract-reviewer agent. Risk flags, missing protections, market-terms assessment, plain-English summary, and recommended actions for a construction contract.

risk_flags.description: Identified risks and unfavorable terms in the contract.

severity.description: critical = potential major financial or legal exposure; high = significant risk; medium = moderate concern; low = minor; info = informational only

category.description: E.g. indemnification, payment_terms, limitation_of_liability, termination, change_orders, etc.

title.description: Short title for this risk flag (max 80 chars).

summary.description: 1-3 sentence plain-English explanation of the risk.

verbatim.description: Verbatim excerpt from the contract that triggers this flag.

why_it_matters.description: Why this risk matters in practical terms for the user.

suggested_action.description: Recommended next step (e.g. 'Ask for mutual indemnity carve-out before signing').

suggested_redline.description: Optional suggested replacement language for this clause.

missing_protections.description: Common protective provisions that are notably absent from this contract.

why_typical.description: Why this protection is standard in Ohio construction contracts.

suggested_clause.description: Short suggested clause language to include.

market_terms_assessment.description: Per-category assessment of whether terms are in-market, off-market-favorable, or off-market-unfavorable. Uses Ohio commercial construction context.

plain_english_summary.description: 200–300 word plain-English summary of the entire agreement, written in chief-of-staff voice. No legalese.

recommended_actions.description: Ordered list of next steps the user should take before signing or relying on this contract.

disclaimer.const: AI-generated analysis. This is not legal advice. Review with qualified counsel before relying on it for any binding decision.

citations.description: Supporting citations from the contract text.

verdict.description: in-market = standard/reasonable; off-market-favorable = better than typical for owner; off-market-unfavorable = worse than typical for owner; not-present = clause or provision absent; unclear = insufficient data to assess

notes.description: 1-3 sentence explanation of the verdict. Acknowledge jurisdiction-specific variation where relevant.
